title: psoralea hypogaea subsp. scaposa
intro: Psoralea hypogaea subsp. scaposa, also known as the "groundhog pea," is a perennial herbaceous legume native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to enhance estrogen production and stimulate the growth of hair and nails. The health benefits of Psoralea hypogaea subsp. scaposa include its potential to treat osteoporosis, improve reproductive health, and reduce the risk of breast cancer. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the seeds in hot water or oil, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote vitality and well-being.
